title: All 3 profile fields hardcoded — genderIdentity, trainingDaysPerWeek, dietPattern never read from UserPreferences
description: ProfileAdapter sets genderIdentity='prefer_not_to_say', trainingDaysPerWeek=compile-time constant, dietPattern='standard' unconditionally. User preferences are completely ignored. Every user gets identical profile bands regardless of their actual profile.
evidence: Three hardcoded assignments in contribute() method
recommended_fix: Read from UserPreferences, fallback to sentinel only when field is nil

title: stressLevel() returns 'moderate' when log is nil — fabricates recovery signal for non-logging users
description: Non-optional return type forces a default. Users who never log stress always get a 'moderate' band sent to cloud, suppressing the insufficientData fallback path for recovery recommendations.
evidence: return "moderate" when no recent log exists
recommended_fix: Return String?, return nil when no log. Propagate nil through contribute() to trigger local fallback.

title: BMI falls back to startWeightKg with no staleness guard or plausibility bounds
description: When live weight is nil, uses onboarding weight (potentially months stale). Additionally, no bounds check — heights of 1cm or weights of 1kg produce wildly wrong BMI values that map to incorrect bands.
evidence: Falls back to profile.startWeightKg, guard heightCm > 0 only
recommended_fix: Return nil when only startWeightKg available. Add plausibility: height 100-250cm, weight 30-300kg.

title: mealsPerDay fallback includes planned-but-uneaten meals — inflates frequency band
description: When no completed meals exist, falls back to latestNutrition?.meals.count which includes planned meals. A user with 5 planned but 0 eaten gets banded as '5+'.
recommended_fix: Return nil when completed count is zero, propagate nil to trigger local fallback

title: Fixed 10min/exercise multiplier — systematic duration undercount, duplicated in weeklyActiveMinutes
description: max(20, exerciseLogs.count * 10) ignores sets, rest periods, logged duration. Same error duplicated in weeklyActiveMinutes calculation. A 6-exercise session estimated at 60min when actual is 75-90min.
evidence: exerciseLogs.count * 10 in both avgSessionMinutes and weeklyActiveMinutes
recommended_fix: Use logged session duration when available, or heuristic based on sets * avg_rest_time

title: JWT format-only validation — expired tokens sent to cloud, wasting network and leaking stale auth
description: looksLikeJWT accepts any x.y.z string. Expired JWTs pass, hit cloud engine, return 401. Wastes network call and may leak request metadata to an endpoint that should have rejected the token pre-flight.
evidence: looksLikeJWT checks format only, no expiry
recommended_fix: Decode JWT payload, check exp claim against current time before sending

title: lastAdapters always empty — setAdapters() never called by any code path, freshness always 0
description: lastAdapters initialized as [] and setAdapters() is never called by any caller. Freshness score always 0, degrading every recommendation's confidence by 0.2 unfairly. This is a dead-code integration point.
evidence: No call to setAdapters() anywhere in the codebase
recommended_fix: Have AISnapshotBuilder.build() return adapters alongside snapshot, wire to orchestrator

title: isProcessing flickers false between segments in processAll() — 3 false→true transitions
description: Each process() call owns isProcessing lifecycle via defer. processAll() triggers 4 process() calls, causing 3 intermediate false states. SwiftUI observers see loading flash.
evidence: defer { isProcessing = false } in process(), called 4x from processAll()
recommended_fix: Lift isProcessing to processAll() scope, pass parameter to suppress in individual calls

title: Foundation Model called without isAvailable check — safety depends on correct DI only
description: No runtime guard before calling foundationModel.adapt(). iOS 26 safety relies entirely on DI injecting FallbackFoundationModel on older iOS. Any DI misconfiguration exposes undefined behavior.
recommended_fix: Add explicit isAvailable guard at call site

title: Adapter disjointness contract is a comment, not enforced — last writer wins silently
description: Multiple adapters can write the same snapshot field. The comment says 'adapters should write disjoint fields' but there's no runtime check. Last adapter to run wins silently.
recommended_fix: Add runtime assertion that no adapter overwrites a field already set by another

title: computeFreshness() uses only newest adapter date — one fresh adapter masks stale sources
description: Freshness is max(adapterDates). If HealthKit updated today but Training last updated 7 days ago, freshness shows 'fresh'. The training recommendations are based on week-old data but appear fresh.
recommended_fix: Use min() or weighted average based on segment relevance

title: buildPrivateContext() assembles full biometrics into loggable plaintext string — PII leak risk at iOS 26 integration
description: Full biometrics (HRV, weight, BMI, sleep hours) assembled into a plaintext prompt string. High probability of PII leak when iOS 26 SDK integration replaces the placeholder with real Foundation Model calls.
recommended_fix: Redact or band-ify values in prompt. Add #warning for iOS 26 integration review.

title: ageBand() returns nil for both under-18 and missing age — silent exclusion indistinguishable
description: No way to differentiate 'user is under 18' from 'age not provided'. No prompt to complete profile.
recommended_fix: Return distinct sentinel or use Result type to differentiate missing vs. excluded

title: workoutConsistency uses hardcoded trainingDaysPerWeek — cascades from DEEP-AI-001
description: Denominator reads the compile-time constant from ProfileAdapter. Consistency band is always wrong.
recommended_fix: Fix flows from DEEP-AI-001 — once ProfileAdapter reads real data, this fixes automatically

title: META: fabrication-over-nil across entire adapter layer inverts confidence signal system-wide
description: Every adapter, when data is missing, injects a plausible sentinel instead of nil. This causes: dataCompleteness scores near 1.0 regardless of real data; three-tier degradation almost never triggers correctly; new users with zero data get high-confidence recommendations; users with real data but expired JWT get low-confidence results. The entire confidence system is inverted.
evidence: Systematic pattern across all 5 adapters: ProfileAdapter hardcodes, HealthKitAdapter fabricates, NutritionAdapter inflates, TrainingAdapter underestimates
recommended_fix: Adopt nil-means-missing contract: adapters return nil for absent data, snapshot marks fields as Optional, dataCompleteness counts non-nil fields, low completeness triggers local-fallback tier correctly
